commit.message:
Fix IDE Ctrl+F9: Create TURBOC3 symlink to match TCDEF.DPR paths

Root cause: TC IDE's TCDEF.DPR has hardcoded paths pointing to
C:\TURBOC3\INCLUDE, C:\TURBOC3\LIB, and C:\TURBOC3\SOURCE, but the
project structure had TC at C:\TC\. The IDE silently failed to find
headers and libraries, causing Ctrl+F9 to fail.

Fix:
- Add TURBOC3 symlink pointing to TC directory
- Update start.sh/start.bat to CD into TURBOC3\BIN before launching TC
- Update TURBOC.CFG to use C:\TURBOC3\ paths for consistency
- Add TC/SOURCE directory (IDE's default source file location)
- Replace TCDEF.DPR and TCDEF.DSK with originals from Turbo C++ 3.2

Verified: TCC compilation, linking, and execution all work through
the TURBOC3 path in DOSBox.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Document that `--show-diff-on-failure --color=always` are always prepended to prek invocation (#75)

Users who add `--show-diff-on-failure` to `extra-args` get a failure
because the action already passes it. The command prek-action runs is
always:

```
prek run --show-diff-on-failure --color=always <extra-args>
```

- **`README.md`**: Added a `[!NOTE]` callout in the Custom Arguments
section stating the fixed flags and warning against duplicating them;
updated the Inputs table description for `extra-args`
- **`action.yaml`**: Updated `extra-args` input description to reflect
that args are appended after `--show-diff-on-failure --color=always`

* feat: auto-migrate deprecated ADO settings to ado.connections (#511)

Add silent, one-time migration during extension activation that converts
legacy ado.organization + ado.projects/ado.project settings into the
unified ado.connections format. The migration is scope-aware (detects
workspace/user/folder level), removes deprecated keys after conversion,
and is tracked via globalState for idempotency.

Also adds deprecationMessage to the three deprecated settings in
package.json so VS Code natively shows deprecation warnings.

- New: src/ado-settings-migration.ts (migration function)
- Modified: src/extension-settings.ts (wire migration into init)
- New: src/__tests__/ado-settings-migration.test.ts (23 test cases)
- Modified: package.json (deprecationMessage fields)

Closes #511

* fix: use workspaceState for migration tracking, don't mark migrated on failure

Address code review findings:
1. globalState → workspaceState: migration tracking is now per-workspace,
   so multi-workspace users get migration for each workspace independently.
2. On config.update() failure, do NOT mark as migrated — allows retry on
   next activation instead of permanently skipping.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: rewrite migration following vscode-jupyter ConfigMigration pattern

Rewrite ADO settings migration based on microsoft/vscode-jupyter's
ConfigMigration.ts — the standard first-party pattern for VS Code
extension settings migration.

Key changes:
- Drop globalState/workspaceState tracking — use inspect() for natural
  idempotency (if old setting exists and new doesn't, migrate)
- Migrate each scope independently (Global, Workspace, WorkspaceFolder)
  so multi-scope users get all their settings migrated
- Chain old-setting removal after new-setting write (Jupyter pattern)
- Clean up orphaned project/projects settings even when org is missing
- Remove context parameter — migration no longer needs ExtensionContext

Reference: microsoft/vscode-jupyter src/platform/common/configMigration.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update VERSION to 2.13.0 with new CHANGELOG entry covering:
- Auto-populate BRIDGE_NAME with hostname
- Cross-bridge sync group visibility
- GitHub Issues infrastructure
- Removal of BRIDGE_NAME_SUFFIX

Add new HISTORY.md / HISTORY.ru.md section for March 8, 2026

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove hop-by-hop Connection header from SSE response

Waitress 3.x strictly enforces PEP 3333 and rejects 'Connection:
keep-alive' as a hop-by-hop header in WSGI responses. This caused
/api/status/stream to crash, breaking real-time status updates in
the Web UI (device cards not rendered).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use correct variable name (status not data) for groups in JS

Both the polling handler and SSE handler referenced 'data.groups'
but the parsed JSON is stored in 'status'. This caused a
ReferenceError that prevented device cards from rendering.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(lxc): mask bluetooth.service, add stop timeout

- install.sh: mask bluetooth.service instead of just disabling it.
  Running bluetoothd inside LXC crashes (no mgmt socket) and can
  break PulseAudio A2DP state if accidentally restarted.
- upgrade.sh: mask bluetooth.service on upgrade for existing installs.
- sendspin-client.service: add TimeoutStopSec=15 and KillMode=mixed
  to prevent indefinite hang during service stop.
- README.md: update docs to reflect masked (not disabled) status.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(api): resolve cross-bridge group enrichment

Two bugs prevented group badge from working:

1. _build_groups_summary() matched Sendspin's group_id (UUID) against
   MA's syncgroup id (syncgroup_XXX). These are different ID systems
   and never matched. Fix: resolve MA syncgroup via player-name mapping
   (get_ma_group_for_player) first, then look up the full group by the
   resolved MA syncgroup id.

2. /api/status for single-device bridges didn't include 'groups' in the
   response (only SSE did). The polling handler in app.js reads
   status.groups, so the badge never rendered on single-device bridges.
   Fix: always append _build_groups_summary() to the status response.

Also enriches group_name from MA API in the groups summary (Sendspin
sends group_name=None).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(hardening): round 10 — 7 weaknesses fixed with TDD

W10: mergeManifestWithExisting shared helper — periodic save no longer
     drops stopped sessions (both periodicManifestSave and performGracefulShutdown
     now use the same DRY helper; stampActive=None vs Some ts controls shutdown stamping)

W11: one-shot Timer (Timeout.Infinite period, reschedule in finally) prevents
     concurrent callbacks; Volatile.Read/Write for lastSavedGeneration ref cell

W12: Volatile.Read on sharedBindingScope reads (HTTP ThreadPool threads);
     Volatile.Write on writer side (MCP server thread) — prevents stale cached reads on ARM

W13: hoist Regex ctor above inner cell loop in BindingExplorer —
     prevents 15-slot static LRU eviction when >15 bindings exist

W14: collect appendEventsAsync tasks in dedup path and Task.WhenAll.Wait(5s)
     consistent with shutdown path (no more fire-and-forget)

W15: rename CRC error 'Header CRC mismatch' → 'File integrity CRC mismatch (whole-file check)'
     — the stored CRC covers the entire file, not just the header

W16: Math.Round for percentile index — int(0.99)=0 but Round(0.99)=1
     so P99 of n=2 now returns the maximum not the minimum

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace TC.EXE with working version and update DOSBox config

CRITICAL FIXES:
1. Replaced TC.EXE (IDE) with working version from Turbo.C.3.2
   - Old MD5: 0fd5acff56c75b0589ff8aa1c752ebe1
   - New MD5: 0ef073f52ac1439bb65ad64d62b25808
   - TCC.EXE and TLINK.EXE were already correct

2. Updated DOSBox configuration to match working Turbo.C.3.2:
   - cycles: 3000 → max (better performance)
   - memsize: 16MB → 32MB (more memory for IDE)
   - output: surface → overlay (fixes display swapping)

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS:
- TurboCPP had Turbo C++ 3.0 IDE (April 2025 date)
- Working installation uses Turbo C++ 3.2 IDE (Nov 2022 date)
- Different TC.EXE versions handle program execution differently
- C0*.OBJ files were necessary but not sufficient
- IDE version + DOSBox config both needed to be fixed

WHY THIS WORKS:
- TC 3.2 has better DOSBox compatibility
- 'overlay' output mode handles screen swapping correctly
- 'max' cycles prevents IDE from being too slow
- 32MB memsize gives IDE more room for buffering

TESTING:
Use TEST_FINAL.C to verify Ctrl+F9 now works:
1. ./start.sh
2. Open TEST_FINAL.C in IDE
3. Press Ctrl+F9
4. Should compile, link, run, and display message

Backup of old TC.EXE saved as TC/BIN/TC.EXE.backup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(calendar): keyboard accessibility (#26)

DatePicker could be opened from keyboard, but focus did not consistently
move into an actionable day cell, making arrow-key navigation unreliable
for keyboard-only users. This change restores deterministic focus
behavior when the popover opens.

- **Focus handoff on open (DatePicker)**
- Defaulted calendar autofocus to `true` in `DatePicker` and forwarded
consumer overrides.
- Ensures opening via keyboard immediately places focus inside the
calendar grid.

- **Day-cell focus wiring (Calendar)**
- Attached the existing `ref` to `CalendarDayButton`’s rendered
`Button`.
- This activates the existing `modifiers.focused` effect so focused days
are actually programmatically focused.

- **Regression coverage (Storybook interaction)**
- Added `KeyboardOnly` story interaction to exercise keyboard-only flow:
    - `Tab` to trigger
    - `Enter` to open
    - initial day button receives focus
    - `ArrowRight` moves focus to another day

```tsx
// date-picker.tsx
const { autoFocus = true, ...restCalendarProps } = calendarProps

<Calendar
  mode="single"
  selected={date}
  onSelect={handleSelect}
  autoFocus={autoFocus}
  {...restCalendarProps}
/>
```

commit.message:
fix(defillama): use edge-triggered dedup to prevent noisy re-alerts

The dedup key included an APY bucket (int(APY)), so when a pool's APY
fluctuated across a 1% boundary (e.g. 17.9% → 18.1%), it generated a
new key and triggered a duplicate alert hours later.

Switch to edge-triggered dedup (same pattern as Merkl/Turtle):
- Dedup key is now pool-only: defillama:<chatID>:<poolID>
- Record permanently when alert fires (pool above APY threshold)
- Clear when pool drops below APY threshold
- This allows re-alerting only on true threshold crossings

Also fetch pools with minAPY=0 so the clearing step can see pools
that dropped below the user's threshold.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: serve tarballs from GitHub Pages to avoid UPM auth failure (#18)

* fix: serve tarballs from GitHub Pages to avoid auth requirement

UPM cannot apply .upmconfig.toml auth to tarball URLs on a different
host than the configured scopedRegistry. GitHub Packages tarballs
always require auth even for public packages, causing 401 errors.

The fix: save the tarball bytes to dist/registry/ alongside the
packument so that GitHub Pages (a public static host) serves both.
UPM resolves packuments and downloads tarballs without any auth.

Changes:
- pages_publisher: accept tarball_data + pages_base_url params;
  write tarball to registry_dir and use Pages URL in packument
- package_publisher: pass tarball_data + PAGES_BASE_URL env to
  PagesPublisher; remove _fetch_github_tarball_url (no longer needed)
- workflow: restore dist/registry from cache before publish (preserves
  tarballs from prior runs); save cache after; set PAGES_BASE_URL env
- tests: replace fetch-tarball-url tests with tarball_data/pages_base_url
  forwarding tests; add TestPagesPublisherTarballStorage class (4 tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: fix line length and black formatting
